title.value: SurveyX: Academic Survey Automation via Large Language Models
title.evidence: SurveyX: Academic Survey Automation via Large Language Models

value: SurveyX significantly enhances the efficacy of survey composition by introducing online reference retrieval, a pre-processing method called AttributeTree, and a re-polishing process.
SurveyX outperforms existing automated survey generation systems in content quality and citation quality, approaching human expert performance.
SurveyX employs retrieval algorithms to search and filter highly relevant references, and uses a reference pre-processing method, termed AttributeTree, to distill key information from the references.
SurveyX extends the evaluation framework by incorporating additional metrics to assess the quality of the generated surveys and retrieved references.
SurveyX's innovative approach includes a two-phase process: the Preparation Phase and the Generation Phase, ensuring a clear structure and accurate content in the generated surveys.
insights.evidence: SurveyX significantly enhances the efficacy of survey composition. Experimental evaluation results show that SurveyX outperforms existing automated survey generation systems in content quality (0.259 improvement) and citation quality (1.76 enhancement), approaching human expert performance across multiple evaluation dimensions.
insights.notes: SurveyX demonstrates significant advancements in automated survey generation, emphasizing its methodological innovations and improved performance metrics.

research_background.value: The exponential growth of literature, as evidenced by the increasing number of papers on arXiv, has made it challenging for researchers to comprehend technological evolution and development in specific subfields. Surveys are crucial for understanding the current state of research and historical progression within a topic.
research_background.evidence: Statistics indicate that arXiv.org receives about a thousand new papers daily. The exponential growth of literature has made it increasingly challenging for researchers attempting to comprehend the technological evolution and developmental trajectories of specific subfields from the ground up.
research_background.notes: The research background highlights the challenge of information overload in academia, necessitating efficient survey generation.

main_contributions.value: SurveyX introduces an efficient reference retrieval algorithm, a reference pre-processing method called AttributeTree, and an outline generation method named Outline Optimization. It also expands the expressive format of generated surveys to include figures and tables, and augments the evaluation framework with additional metrics.
main_contributions.evidence: We propose an efficient reference retrieval algorithm capable of expanding keywords based on a given topic. We design a reference pre-processing method called AttributeTree. We introduce an outline generation method named Outline Optimization. We expand the expressive format of the generated survey to include figures and tables.
main_contributions.notes: SurveyX's contributions focus on improving retrieval, processing, and presentation of survey content.

core_approach.value: SurveyX divides the composing process into two phases: Preparation and Generation. The Preparation Phase involves retrieval and pre-processing of references using AttributeTree. The Generation Phase focuses on creating the survey's outline and main body, incorporating tables and figures, and refining the draft with RAG-based rewriting and multimodal models.
core_approach.evidence: SurveyX divides the composing process of surveys into two phases: the Preparation Phase and the Generation Phase. In the Preparation Phase, SurveyX employs retrieval algorithms to search and filter highly relevant references. In the Generation Phase, SurveyX utilizes the information obtained in the previous phase to sequentially generate the outline and main body of the survey.
core_approach.notes: The core approach involves a structured two-phase process for efficient survey generation.

reference_acquisition.value: SurveyX uses both offline and online data sources for reference acquisition. The offline source consists of papers from arXiv.org, while the online source utilizes a self-developed crawler system based on Google Scholar. The retrieval algorithm includes a Keyword Expansion Algorithm to ensure comprehensive recall and a 2-step filtration algorithm for relevance.
reference_acquisition.evidence: The retrieval data source includes both offline and online data sources. The offline data source consists of available papers downloaded from the arXiv.org. The online data source is a self-developed crawler system based on Google Scholar. The retrieval algorithm is divided into two steps: reference recall and reference filter.
reference_acquisition.notes: The acquisition strategy ensures timely and relevant reference collection through diverse sources and algorithms.

retrieval_algorithms.value: The retrieval algorithm involves a Keyword Expansion Algorithm to expand keywords and ensure comprehensive recall of relevant references. It uses semantic clustering to identify and add the most appropriate keywords to the pool. The 2-step filtration algorithm uses an embedding model for coarse-grained filtration and LLMs for fine-grained filtration.
retrieval_algorithms.evidence: The retrieval algorithm is divided into two steps: reference recall and reference filter. We design a method called the Keyword Expansion Algorithm. The pseudo code can be found at Algorithm 1. The Select function, when choosing candidate words, assumes that the best candidate word should have the smallest possible average distance to the existing keywords.
retrieval_algorithms.notes: The algorithms enhance retrieval efficiency and relevance through advanced keyword expansion and filtration techniques.

reference_preprocessing.value: SurveyX uses a method called AttributeTree for reference pre-processing. This involves designing attribute tree templates for different types of references to efficiently extract key information. The attribute trees are combined into an attribute forest, representing all reference materials required for survey composition.
reference_preprocessing.evidence: We design a reference pre-processing method called AttributeTree. Specifically, we designed different attribute tree templates for different types of references in advance. The attribute trees of all references are combined into an attribute forest.
reference_preprocessing.notes: AttributeTree enhances information density and context window utilization for high-quality survey composition.

outline_generation.value: Outline generation involves the Outline Optimization method, which generates outlines based on hints from the attribute tree. This method includes a 'separate-then-reorganize' step to eliminate redundancy, resulting in outlines with rigorous logic and clear structure.
outline_generation.evidence: We designed an outline generation method named Outline Optimization. This method consists of two steps. For the first step, this method initially lets LLMs generate hints corresponding to the secondary outline based on the attribute tree of each reference.
outline_generation.notes: The method ensures logical rigor and clear structure in survey outlines through strategic hint-based generation.

content_generation.value: Content generation uses a hint-based method derived from the secondary outline to guide the generation of the main body content. LLMs generate content on a subsection basis, ensuring consistency across subsections.
content_generation.evidence: During the content generation stage, inspired by the human writing process for surveys, we continue to use the hint-based method employed during the outline generation stage to enhance the depth of the generated content.
content_generation.notes: The method enhances content depth and consistency by leveraging hints from the outline generation stage.

post_optimization.value: Post optimization involves a RAG-based rewriting module to improve content quality and citation accuracy. It also includes a figure and table generation module to enrich survey presentation, using information extraction templates and MLLMs.
post_optimization.evidence: Based on this, we design a post refinement stage, which polishes the initial draft with two main goals: (1) to improve the quality of the content, including citation quality, textual fluency, and consistency of expression; (2) to add figures and tables to enrich the presentation of the survey.
post_optimization.notes: Post optimization enhances both the content quality and presentation diversity of the generated surveys.
